Serial debugging functions for initialization, string output, and formatted printing.

# Purpose
This header file declares a set of functions for serial debugging output. The function [`dbgserial_init`](<#dbgserial_init>) initializes the serial debugging interface. The [`dbgserial_putstr`](<#dbgserial_putstr>) and [`dbgserial_print`](<#dbgserial_print>) functions send a string to the serial output, with [`dbgserial_print`](<#dbgserial_print>) omitting a terminating newline. The [`dbgserial_newline`](<#dbgserial_newline>) function sends a newline character to the serial output. The [`dbgserial_print_hex`](<#dbgserial_print_hex>) function outputs a 32-bit unsigned integer in hexadecimal format. The [`dbgserial_putstr_fmt`](<#dbgserial_putstr_fmt>) function formats a string according to a specified format and variable arguments, similar to `printf`, and writes it to a buffer. The file includes standard headers `stdarg.h` and `stdint.h` for handling variable arguments and fixed-width integer types, respectively.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdarg.h`
- `stdint.h`
